name: Lattice-Top Linzertorte
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust rack to the lower third of the oven; preheat to 325 degrees F.
2. Sift flour, cinnamon, salt, and baking powder onto a strip of waxed paper.
3. In a large bowl, combine butter and sugar; beat until smooth.
4. Add egg and beat until well blended.
5. Stir in flour mixture, nuts, and lemon zest just until thoroughly blended.
6. Divide dough into two portions.
7. Using floured fingertips, pat half of the dough evenly into an ungreased 9-inch square baking pan.
8. Spread jam evenly over the dough, stopping 1/2-inch from the edges.
9. Set aside.
10. Roll remaining dough between 2 pieces of waxed paper to a 9-inch square; place on a baking sheet and refrigerate until firm.
11. Cut dough into 14 strips, each 1/2 inch wide.
12. Evenly space 7 of the strips over the torte in one direction.
13. Place remaining strips at right angles to the first strips, creating a lattice pattern.
14. Bake for 45 minutes, or until light golden.
15. Cool completely before cutting into 2-inch squares.
Yields 16 squares. Per Square: 225 calories, 3 g protein, 23 g carbohydrate, 14 g fat (5 g saturated), 33 mg cholesterol, 44 mg sodium, 1 g fiber.
